42: As you well know, the manufactory here is run by House Haillenarte. From cannons to ballistas, we develop an' manufacture a range of weapons what are made for slayin' dragon invaders.

43: We also dabble in machinistry─that's a technology what combines Master Garlond's magitek with traditional aetherology. All our machinist firearms are built on its principles.

44: Of course, it's Lord Stephanivien as comes up with the designs. The bloke's a bloody genius...even if he is a bit odd.

45: He ain't like any noble I've ever met. As long as you've got a desire to learn the machinist trade, he's as happy to recruit the poorest Brume rat as he is the richest highborn.
As you well know, the manufactory here is run by House Haillenarte. From cannons to ballistas, we develop an' manufacture a range of weapons what are made for slayin' dragon invaders.

46: As his lordship puts it, “With the trainin' of a machinist, an' the flexibility of a firearm, a skilled commoner can stand on equal footing with the mightiest knight.”
We also dabble in machinistry─that's a technology what combines Master Garlond's magitek with traditional aetherology. All our machinist firearms are built on its principles.

47: Of course, givin' a lowborn bloke like me a voice equal to a privileged noble ain't as easy as slappin' a carbine in me hand. But as far as grantin' us commonfolk a bit of independence goes, it's a step in the right direction.

51: Basically, a machinist is someone what wields a firearm. But it's the aetherotransformer worn at <If(PlayerParameter(4))>her<Else/>his</If> waist what makes <If(PlayerParameter(4))>her<Else/>him</If> different from other marksmen. This machine converts the aether found within a person's body into lightnin'-aspected energy, which then powers the machinist's arsenal of specialized devices an' techniques.
